---
title: "Mini Project 3: Data Viz Re-Design"
subtitle: "If you want a subtitle, put it here"
author: John Helveston - Update with your name
date: September 05, 2026 - Update with your submission date
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    warning = FALSE,
    message = FALSE,
    fig.path = "figs/", # Folder where rendered plots are saved
    fig.width = 7.252, # Default plot width
    fig.height = 4, # Default plot height
    fig.retina = 3, # For better plot resolution
    comment = "#>"
)

# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(here)
library(janitor)
```

<!-- Delete all of this scaffolding text before submitting -- it's just
here as a guide. -->

# Load the Data

<!-- Step 2: have Claude Code read in US_State_Wind_Energy_Facts_2018.xlsx
from the data folder. -->

# Preview the Data

<!-- Step 3: look at the data yourself first -- head(), glimpse(), check
whether numeric columns are actually encoded as numbers. -->

# Clean the Data

<!-- Step 4: have Claude Code clean up variable types and names (janitor::
clean_names() is handy). Write a few sentences on what you changed and
why. -->

# Plan Your Approach

<!-- Step 5: BEFORE generating any charts, compute your summary measures
(centrality/variability + at least 2 new "leadership" measures), and
write down -- for each of your two planned charts -- which metric it
highlights, why that's a meaningful kind of leadership, and what chart
type you'll use and why. -->

# Visualizations

<!-- Step 6: direct Claude Code to build your two charts here. They must
use genuinely different chart styles from each other. Claude Code should
edit this file directly. -->

# Critique Your Redesign

<!-- Step 7: check both charts for new distortions (truncated axes,
misleading sorting/color), recompute one under an alternative cut of the
data, and name at least one real limitation of your own redesign. -->

# Summary

<!-- Step 8: what was wrong with the original chart (name specific design
principles), what your two redesigns improve and convey, and what your
Step 7 critique changed or still leaves as a caveat. -->

<!-- Step 9: once you've rendered and proofread report.html, run /export
in Claude Code and save it as transcript.md in this folder before you zip
everything up for submission. -->
